expectations.text: Output contains at least 10 distinct hooks
No hooks start with 'Are you...' (tired generic AI ad opener)
expectations.evidence: none of the banned patterns appeared
none of the banned patterns appeared

expectations.text: Hooks include concrete specifics (numbers, names, objects, situations) not vague claims
expectations.evidence: 47 permission layers, 14 clicks, 200 features/6 used/194 resented, 8-person studio, 11 people

expectations.text: Output uses VARIETY of psychological trigger types (not all the same category)
expectations.evidence: Reason-why reframe, math/calculation, disqualification, identity flip, absurdity, enemy-naming

expectations.text: Hooks feel native to the given persona's voice/context (not generic ad copy)
expectations.evidence: Creative director, 8-person studio, designers, Slack+Figma workflow — boutique-agency native

expectations.text: Hooks specifically reference Asana/Monday by name OR concrete UX gripes (clicking, permissions, 47 layers)
expectations.evidence: Asana (5x), Monday.com named, 47 permission layers, 14 clicks, 200 features

expectations.text: Hooks differentiate (show what competitors got wrong), not just claim to be better
expectations.evidence: Every hook attacks competitor's wrong design premise; no self-praise claims
